hypothesis: After recent capital-allocation improvements, the same-day per-sector cap may be mis-sized: relaxing it could capture sector leadership clusters, while tightening it could reduce correlated false breakouts.
change_summary: Ran an experiment-only replay that tested MAX_PER_SECTOR=1 and MAX_PER_SECTOR=3 against the current baseline of 2 across the fixed backtesting.md windows.

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull where strategy makes money but lags indexes
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with lower win rate

delta_metrics.gate4_basis: Rejected because tightening damaged all three windows and relaxing to 3 was a strict null.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft ranking remains sample-limited, so this deterministic allocation alpha was tested instead.

rejection_reason: The current same-day sector cap of 2 remains the best tested global shape: a cap of 1 removes profitable clustered exposure, while a cap of 3 changes nothing under current slot competition.
next_retry_requires: Do not retry nearby global sector cap values without new sector-leadership evidence.
A valid retry needs a state- or sleeve-specific sector allocation signal, not a global cap.
Do not confuse this with residual sector pocket sizing rules already accepted or rejected.
notes: Alpha search, not bug repair. Production strategy code was unchanged because Gate 4 failed.
